reports.text:
WAR IMPACT: SECTOR-WISE SUMMARY  

KEY NOTE  
- Prolonged war (>2 months) may disrupt supply chains & cost pass-through  
- Short-term inventory gains possible; long-term margin pressure  

OIL & GAS (O&G)  
- Upstream: Beneficiaries of higher crude prices  
- Downstream (OMCs): Worst hit; limited pricing power  
- LNG demand shifts may impact India’s gas economics  

INFRA  
- Potential slowdown in govt capex  
- Middle-East execution/order inflow may weaken  

AIRLINES  
- ATF = 35–40% of costs; 10% rise cuts ~1.5% EBITDA margin  
- Flight rerouting & conflict impact earnings visibility  

FMCG  
- Crude-linked packaging = ~15% cost  
- 2–3 months inventory; near-term margin pressure  
- Large players better placed to pass on costs  

ALCOHOL  
- Gas-intensive production; 20–25% gas price rise  
- Overall cost increase of ~3–5%  

TEXTILES  
- MEG/PTA supply disruption (polyester inputs)  
- Polyester yarn prices ↑ ₹20–25/kg (15–20%)  
- Inventory gains near term; cotton yarn players benefit  

CEMENT  
- No ME supply risk; but fuel cost impact  
- 10% fuel price rise → ~₹13–14/t EBITDA hit  
- Packaging cost inflation adds ~₹8/t impact  

HOME IMPROVEMENT  
- Crude-linked inputs → margin hit 70–150 bps  
- Wood panels: 30–60 bps decline  
- Pipes: minimal impact (pass-through model)  

SUGAR  
- Ethanol diversion ↑ → sugar prices +1.5%  
- Higher crude may accelerate ethanol blending  

FERTILIZERS  
- Input & logistics shock; supply stable for now  
- Urea: cost ↑ but subsidy offset likely  
- Positive: domestic players; Negative: DAP importers  

CHEMICALS  
- Crude-linked input inflation across segments  
- Export-oriented/FMCG-linked players face margin pressure  
- Specialty players with pass-through contracts relatively safe  

STEEL  
- Export realization benefit from global price rise  
- Slight input cost increase (coal/scrap)  
- Near-term margin tailwinds  

AUTO  
- Margins largely stable; minor cost pressure  
- Demand impact may come with lag if inflation persists  

RAC & EMS  
- Temporary disruption due to LPG/resin shortages  
- AC production may be impacted in peak summer  
- EMS exports stable for now; risk if disruption prolongs  

OVERALL TAKE  
- Winners: Upstream O&G, Steel (short-term), Cotton yarn  
- Losers: OMCs, Airlines, FMCG (near-term), Chemicals (margin pressure)  
- Neutral/Mixed: Auto, Cement, EMS, Fertilizers

Source : DAM CAPITAL

POSITIVE FOR PETRONET LNG? 

Seems like Indian PLNG vol is not impacted by 2 damaged train.

Qatar has venture with foreign partners like Exxon shell total etc. in each venture there can be 1 or more LNG trains out of 14 trains.

And each venture has sold volume to long term basis to different customers. 

India PLNG vol is through train 3 which is not damaged. 

Qatar can't transfer volume from 1 venture to another venture and it outage vol can't be proportion among 77mt capacity.

Not a recommendation

Saudi Arabia is ramping up oil exports amid the Strait of Hormuz closure:

Crude oil shipments from Yanbu, a port on Saudi Arabia's Red Sea coast, are up to 4.19 million barrels per day.

This marks a +185% increase from the ~1.47 million barrels per day that moved through the port in February, before the Iran War.

The surge is being powered by a 746-mile pipeline rerouting crude from the eastern oil fields to Yanbu, bypassing the blocked Strait of Hormuz entirely.

Shipments have also more than doubled since January’s 1.29 million barrels per day.

As a result, Saudi Arabia has already recovered more than half of its pre-war export capacity of ~7 million barrels per day.

Furthermore, at least 32 large oil tankers are waiting near Yanbu to load, with more still heading to the port.

Saudi Arabia is aggressively looking to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

Russian Oil's Journey to India

India's Key Role  
India now imports over one-third of its crude from Russia.  
Most cargoes from Baltic (Primorsk, Ust-Luga) and Black Sea (Novorossiysk) ports.  
Primarily Urals crude, shipped via Aframax and Suezmax tankers.

Main Shipping Route  
Baltic/Black Sea → Mediterranean → Suez Canal → Red Sea → Bab El Mandeb → Arabian Sea → India.  
Standard voyage: 30-40 days.  
Red Sea tensions occasionally force Cape of Good Hope rerouting.

Economic Drivers  
Price discounts on Urals vs. Middle East sour grades drive imports.  
Indian refiners prioritize economics over shorter voyages.  
Shadow fleet and ship-to-ship transfers bypass sanctions/insurance hurdles.

Trade Outlook  
Attractive pricing ensures India remains prime destination.  
Pragmatic refiners sustain flows despite logistics challenges.

China Suspends Fertilizer Exports – Global Agriculture Shock

Breaking Development  
China halts all overseas shipments of fertilizer blends.  
Instructed exporters to suspend nitrogen-potassium blends and urea.  
World's largest supplier prioritizing domestic needs amid Middle East war disruptions.

Impacted Regions  
- US farmers face acute shortages for spring planting.  
- Australia: 70-75% ammonium phosphate imports; nearly 100% urea from Middle East, China, SE Asia.  
- Tight planting schedules at risk in both countries.

Market Consequences  
- Crop production costs to surge sharply.  
- Global food prices (wheat, corn, soy) expected to rise.  
- Supply chain ripples into livestock feed, energy-intensive production.

Geopolitical Leverage  
- Highlights China's dominance in nitrogen, phosphate, potassium fertilizers.  
- Dependent nations scramble for alternatives or face yield drops.  
- War in Iran exacerbates via Hormuz Strait blockages on raw materials.

Energy Data Now National Security Priority

New Government Mandate  
Government classifies energy data as national security matter.  
Requires full disclosure across entire oil & gas value chain.  
Issued via Petroleum and Natural Gas (Furnishing of Information) Order, 2026 (March 18 gazette).  

Who Must Report  
- Refiners, LNG importers, pipeline operators  
- City gas distributors, petrochemical firms  
- Both public and private sector entities  
- Reports to Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC)  

Data Requirements  
- Granular operational details: production, imports, stocks, consumption  
- Frequency: Regular reporting, daily in critical cases  
- Overrides existing confidentiality provisions  

Security Context

WELSPUN LIVING LTD – KEY UPDATES  

Acquisition of Stake in WCSL  
- Board approves acquisition of 3,500 equity shares (35%) in Welspun Corporate Services Limited (WCSL) for ₹35,000; balance 65% via other Welspun Group entities.  
- WCSL to act as centralized corporate services platform for the group for HR, legal, tax and strategic advisory.  
- WCSL will become an associate company of Welspun Living with a joint shareholders’ agreement; no change in management and control.  
- Transaction expected by March 31, 2026; treat as related‑party, arm’s‑length move.  

Management Changes  
- Mr. Rajesh Jain steps down as CHRO and Senior Management Team member, effective March 31, 2026.  
- Ms. Sonia Sharma appointed Vice President – Human Resource, effective April 1, 2026, and joins the Senior Management Team.  
- Ms. Sharma brings 20+ years in the Welspun Group, with strong experience in strategic HR and organisational transformation and is not related to any Director.  

Capex Increase for Renewable Power Transmission  
- Capex for RE power transmission to Anjar facility raised from ₹75 crore to ₹159.38 crore.  
- Scope includes CTU connectivity, land acquisition, sub‑station bay, transmission line erection and inner connectivity ring changes.

**Power Sector Update – Demand & Pricing Trends**

**Demand Trends**
• February 2026 demand grew modestly at +1.5% YoY
• Early March (1–17) shows revival with +3.4% YoY growth

**Tariff Movement**
• DAM tariffs averaged ₹3.58/kWh in Feb’26 (↓ 18% YoY)
• March MTD tariffs rebounded to ₹4.55/kWh (↑ 4% YoY)
• Improvement driven by pickup in demand

**Capacity & Renewable Mix**
• Installed capacity reached 524 GW as of Feb’26
• Record addition of 54 GW over last 12 months
• Renewables contributed 100% of recent capacity addition
• RE share increased to 41% (vs 36% YoY)

**Overall View**
• Short-term demand softness seen in Feb, but recovery visible in March
• Rising renewable share continues to reshape power mix
• Positive demand outlook with improving pricing trend

EMKAY ON HDFC BANK

Post the conference call with the management we are completely assured that there are no corporate governance issues in HDFC Bank

We are not completely assured that there are no operational issues in the bank

There seems to be some differences of opinion at the management level leading to these ruffles

We see that it will take some time for the bank to settle all such internal issues

We have not changed any of our estimates so we continue to retain our BUY rating on HDFC Bank

However, it has been low in our pecking order and we continue to prefer ICICI Bank, Axis Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank

China’s demand for gold remains robust:

China officially bought 1 tonne of gold in February, marking the 16th consecutive monthly purchase.

Over this period, China has acquired 50 tonnes of gold. 

By comparison, in 2022-2024, the buying streak lasted for 18 straight months. 

As a result, China’s total gold reserves are up to a record 2,309 tonnes, or 10% of foreign exchange reserves.

The percentage has DOUBLED over the last 20 months.

At the current gold price of ~$5,000/oz, China now owns ~$371 billion of gold.

China is holding more gold than ever.

As per data, timing the market is extremely risky.

If you miss a few of the best days of the market, you lose a lot of return.

**KHAZANCHI JEWELLERS: B2B → B2C SHIFT DRIVING A STRUCTURAL MARGIN UPGRADE**

Most investors focus on revenue growth.
Smart money tracks where the margin pool is shifting. 📊

**Khazanchi is moving from a wholesale-led B2B model to a retail-led B2C model — and the numbers explain why this matters.**

In B2B, margins are capped at ~4.5–5% despite scale.
In B2C, margins are already at ~9–10% and expected to expand to 12–13% with premium and branded jewellery mix. 💎

**That’s a 2–3x margin expansion opportunity without changing the core product**

The transition is visible:
B2C contribution is expected to rise from single-digit levels to ~20–25% by FY27, shifting the earnings engine 📈

Execution is backing the story:
A 10,000 sq ft flagship showroom with ~₹550 Cr revenue potential acts as a key catalyst for retail scale 🏬

**Now the real insight:**

Higher B2C mix → Higher margins
Higher margins → Better return ratios
Better return ratios → Valuation re-rating

Same gold.
But a completely different business model

Yesterday, Adani bought Jaypee Group for Rs 14,535 Cr 

But Vedanta had bid around Rs 17,000Cr, over Rs 2,000 Cr MORE than Adani

Yet their higher bid lost and Adani won the bid despite a lower bid!

So how did the lowest bid win one of India's largest bankruptcy deals?

The lenders reasoned that this was because of a higher upfront payment and faster timeline

Vedanta called this unfair and challenged it at NCLT, but got rejected yesterday

But here's the thing, this wasn't really the "lenders" deciding 

It was actually NARCL

So when the time came to choose between Adani and Vedanta, it wasn't really a "committee" deciding

It was effectively one government-backed entity with 86% of the vote

🚨 PVR CINEMAS REVENUE MODEL.

If you pay ₹200 for a ticket, how much does the theatre keep?

About 50%. The other 50% goes to the movie producer.

The split changes based on performance.
If the film doesn’t hit the required numbers, PVR gets a 2.5% bonus.

So the split becomes:
PVR: 52.5%
Producer: 47.5%

If the film performs well, the producer earns more in later weeks.

The theatre’s share can drop to:

37.5% in the next week
and then ~30% later.

So the bigger the film runs,
the more money shifts to the producer per ticket.

Apple Surpasses PLI Targets with Strong iPhone Production in India

Apple has exceeded its PLI scheme production target by ~80%

Against a target of ₹3.35 lakh crore, actual iPhone production reached ₹6.02 lakh crore

Production between FY21–FY26 driven by exports and supply chain shift from China to India

FY26 production alone estimated at ₹2.2 lakh crore, up sharply YoY

Growth accelerated after Tata Electronics and Foxconn ramped up manufacturing

iPhones are now being produced at multiple large-scale facilities in India

India’s share in global iPhone production has risen to ~25%

iPhone production grew ~24% CAGR (FY25–FY26)

Apple reached the minimum investment threshold early, making it ineligible for extra incentives on additional output

PLI scheme has helped position India as a global export hub for electronics

However, most competitors under PLI have focused more on domestic market vs exports

India Plans ₹1,800 Cr Scheme to Boost Drone Manufacturing

Government is planning a ₹1,600–1,800 crore incentive scheme (Mission Drone Shakti)

Aim is to boost domestic drone manufacturing and reduce dependence on Chinese imports

Scheme likely to be rolled out in FY27, targeting private Indian companies

**THE REAL OIL POWER IS REFINING, NOT JUST PRODUCTION**

**GLOBAL REFINING CAPACITY SNAPSHOT**
• China around 18.5 mb per day largest refining system globally
• United States around 18.4 mb per day highly complex and flexible
• Russia around 6.8 mb per day
• India around 5.2 mb per day
• Saudi Arabia around 3.3 mb per day

**KEY INSIGHT**
• Refining capacity determines real control over fuel supply chain
• Countries with high refining can convert crude into high value products

**STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE**
• Refining adds value versus just exporting crude
• Controls pricing power in petrol diesel aviation fuel and petrochemicals
• Critical for energy security and trade balance

**INDIA POSITIONING**
• India among top global refiners
• Strong export oriented refining model
• Advantage in complex refining and product mix

**BIG PICTURE TAKEAWAY**
• Oil power is shifting from resource ownership to processing capability
• Refining scale plus complexity defines global energy influence

- **Core growth thesis unchanged**; long-term prospects seen **robust** despite temporary delays.

**Gradual Improvement in Growth Outlook**  
- Demand in Q4 remains healthy; AUM growth seen at **16–18% in FY27E**.  
- **NIMs expected to stay steady** despite a slight rise in marginal cost of funds.  
- **Collections improving**, leading to credit cost easing to **~2.4% in near term**, with further moderation ahead.  

**Profitability Set to Strengthen**  
- Management expects **ROA to reach ~2.5% by FY28**.  
- **Valuations look reasonable** after recent stock pullback.  

**Re-rating Triggers:** Sustained **growth momentum** and **credit cost decline**.  

**Key Takeaway:** Jefferies sees HDB Financial maintaining steady fundamentals with improving growth and profitability, making it a potential re-rating candidate.

**🇮🇳 INDIA SEMICONDUCTOR SECTOR – QUICK INVESTMENT SNAPSHOT**

**🧭 OVERALL VIEW**
• Early-stage capex-led upcycle
• Strong tailwinds from AI + electronics + policy
• Still import dependent + execution risk
• Strategy = selective play, not broad betting

**📊 DEMAND DRIVERS**
**Short Term (2–3 yrs)**
• Smartphones + electronics manufacturing boom
• 5G rollout + data centre expansion (AI infra push)
• China+1 shift (Apple, global OEMs)

**Long Term (5–10 yrs)**
• AI devices → higher chip content per device
• EVs + auto electronics + defence demand
• Cloud + AI ecosystem expansion

**🏭 SUPPLY SIDE POSITIONING**
• Strong in design (20% global talent)
• Emerging in ATMP/OSAT (Micron, Tata, CG Power JV)
• Weak in fabs & materials (import heavy)
• Focus = mature nodes (28–65nm), not advanced nodes

**🏗 KEY SEGMENT OPPORTUNITIES**
**Fabs / OSAT (Manufacturing)**
• High capex + high operating leverage
• Beneficiaries: Tata Electronics, Micron, CG Power JV
• Risk: execution + utilisation

**Fabless / IP (Best ROI Segment)**
• Asset-light, high margin
• Focus areas: edge-AI, EV chips, RF, analog
• Strong startup + design ecosystem

**Materials / Equipment (Import Substitution)**
• Huge opportunity (90% import dependency)
• Early stage → long runway
• Policy support via ECMS

**💰 MARGIN DRIVERS**
• Govt subsidy (up to 50% capex support)
• Operating leverage (utilisation ramp-up)
• Product mix (IP > manufacturing margins)
• Raw material import dependency (key risk)

**⚡️ KEY STRUCTURAL TRENDS**
• Shift to compute-driven AI economy
• Supply chain becoming security-driven (not cost-driven)
• India evolving → design → packaging → partial manufacturing
• Policy shift → ecosystem + IP creation focus

**🏛 POLICY SUPPORT**
• ₹76,000 Cr ISM + ₹40,000 Cr ECMS
• 50% subsidy for fabs
• Design incentives (DLI: 4–6% sales support)
• Tax benefits + talent push (85,000 VLSI engineers/year target)

**🌍 GLOBAL CONTEXT**
• Taiwan dominates advanced chips
• US leads design/IP
• China controls materials
• India = design + backend hub emerging

**📈 GROWTH OUTLOOK (2–3 YEARS)**
• Market growth: ~15–18% CAGR
• Early gains from ATMP/OSAT ramp-up
• Fabless design companies gaining traction
• Capex pipeline strong ($15B+ committed)

**⚠️ KEY RISKS**
• Execution delays (infra, fabs)
• High import dependency
• Low R&D spend (~0.6% GDP)
• Talent shortage / brain drain
• Future overcapacity risk

**🎯 INVESTMENT STRATEGY (MOST IMPORTANT)**
**Best Bet (High Conviction)**
• Fabless / IP companies (high margin, scalable)

**Second Layer**
• OSAT / ATMP players (operating leverage play)

**Early Stage Bets**
• Materials & equipment (import substitution theme)

**Avoid / Be Cautious**
• Low value assembly players
• Overhyped fab stories (long gestation, low ROCE risk)

**🧠 ONE LINE TAKE**
• India semiconductor = Design + Packaging story now, Manufacturing story later 🚀

**OIL SHOCK: NIFTY 15-20% RISK**  

**Thesis**  
- $25-30/bbl spike hits GDP 1-2pp, EPS 12%→5-7%, P/E 20x→17-18x.  
- Cyclical; digital/manuf/energy drives rebound.  

**Drivers**  
- GDP 6.5-6.7% to #3 by FY31.  
- Datacenters #2 APAC; EVs/H2/nuclear cut oil use.  
- Exports/domestic buffer.  

**Sectors**  
- **Severe neg**: OMCs (under-recoveries), aviation (ATF).  
- **Neg**: Financials (credit 7-8%), auto/chemicals/paints.  
- **Mild neg**: FMCG.  
- **Pos**: Upstream O&G, pharma, defence, digital infra.  

**Commentary**  
- $10/bbl = 0.2pp GDP drag; RBI delay 2-4Q.  

**Financials**  
- Crude bill $130-140 Bn; Nifty stress P/E 17-18x.  

**Valuations**  
- Normal 20-22x; post-bottom 90%+ gains.  

**Risks**  
- Crude persistence, rupee inflation, fiscal, China/Russia.  

**Triggers**  
- **Near**: Oil stabilise, rupee/RBI/yields.  
- **Med**: Credit revival, structural wins.  

**Bull/Bear**  
- **Bear**: 15-20% drop.  
- **Bull**: Quick peak →90% rally.  

**Key Takeaway:** Buy upstream/digital on weakness; await oil peak.

**KOTAK EPIC CHURN: DISTILLED EXTRACTION**  

**1. Investment Thesis**  
- Conflict correction = churn to quality, dump narratives/expensive.  
- Temporary crude hit; structural higher CoE leads 1-3Y derating.  

**2. Growth Drivers**  
- Nifty EPS CAGR: 8.2% FY26E, 16.2% FY27E, 14.8% FY28E (banks/oil/IT/metals).  
- GDP 7%/7.6%/6.8%; upstream/refiners gain short-term.  

**3. Segment Outlook**  
- **Financials**: Tier-2/3 banks/NBFCs cheap P/B.  
- **Consumption**: FMCG/QSR/retail/paints rich fwd P/E – reduce.  
- **Oil&Gas**: Upstream/refiners up; OMCs down.  
- **PSUs/Capgoods**: HAL/BHEL/IRFC/NHPC overvalued vs earnings/NW.  

**4. Broker View**  
- Base: Weeks conflict, months tension; SoH normalizes soon.  
- 1-2Q blips overpriced at 10-15% cuts.  

**5. Financials**  
- Nifty EPS: FY26E ₹1,072, FY27E ₹1,246, FY28E ₹1,432.  
- $10/bbl crude = $20 Bn CAD (0.5% GDP).  

**6. Valuations**  
- Nifty 19x FY27E, 16.5x FY28E.  
- BJFIN FV ₹2,500 (+41%); Eternal ₹375 (+70%).  

**7. Risks**  
- Prolonged SoH disruption/high crude/CAD strain.  
- Elections limit OMC pricing; faster derating.  

**8. Triggers**  
- **Near**: Conflict evolution, govt fuel stance, corp gas commentary.  
- **Medium**: Rates/disruption visibility, earnings delivery.  

**9. Bull/Bear**  
- **Bull**: Quick resolve, buy dips in quality (mid-teens Nifty growth).  
- **Bear**: Prolonged energy shock, broad derating lags earnings.  

**Key Takeaway:** Churn now: Financials in, consumption/PSUs out.

**The Real Story — It's Not Nifty, It's Your Portfolio**

**The Ground Reality**
- Stocks analyzed: **1,976**
- Median stock down: **36.4%** from its high
- Microcap median drawdown: **44.4%**

**What This Is — And What It Isn't**
- This is **NOT** a 2008-style meltdown
- This is a **"Quality Sale"** — froth being cleansed
- A **historic but healthy** market correction

**Why You're Not Doing Anything Wrong**
- Nifty looks stable — but **most portfolios are bleeding**
- Drawdowns of **30–45%** are widespread — not isolated
- Feeling lost right now = **completely normal**

**The Wealth-Building Moment**
- Next **5 years of wealth** will be built **right here**
- The edge belongs to those who **sit through the pain**
- One click of **'Sell'** can cost years of compounding

**The Investor Mindset Right Now**
- Don't benchmark against **Nifty**
- Benchmark against **your own buy price & thesis**
- If fundamentals are intact — **drawdown is just noise**

**Key Takeaway**
- **36% median drawdown** = rare entry opportunity in quality names
- Fear is the **highest at the best buying points**
- The market is not punishing you — it's **testing your conviction**

**Sahana System: Positioned for Growth in Anti-Drone Defense Amid Global Tensions**

Sahana System Limited is strategically expanding into the anti-drone (Counter-UAS) segment, aligning with rising global demand for advanced defense technologies.

With modern warfare increasingly driven by drone-based threats, governments are accelerating investments in detection, jamming and electronic warfare systems.

Sahana is capitalizing on this shift through RF and radar-based detection, AI-powered threat intelligence and integrated counter-drone solutions.

The company’s recent ₹24.69 crore order from Central Electronics Limited highlights strong execution and entry into defense electronics.

Partnership with Bharat Electronics Limited strengthens its position within India’s defense ecosystem.

Increasing demand from military, border security and critical infrastructure protection continues to support growth.

As geopolitical tensions persist, anti-drone technology is emerging as a critical defense priority.

**BOFA GLOBAL FUND MANAGER SURVEY – BEARISH TURN**  

**Sentiment Shift**  
- **March FMS turns bearish** amid Iran tensions and private credit concerns.  
- Ends the **"frothy bull" sentiment** of recent months.  

**Key Metrics**  
- **Cash levels surge** to **4.2%**; growth optimism sharply declines.  
- **No equity capitulation** – positioning far from uber-bear extremes.  

**Trade Signals**  
- **Sell oil above $100/bbl**, **sell DXY above 100**.  
- **Buy GT30 at 5%**, **buy SPX at 6600**.  

**Market Implications**  
- Metrics **not at big lows** seen at optimal stock/credit entry points.  

**Key Takeaway:** Bearish tilt without capitulation suggests **selective buying opportunities** in equities and credit remain intact.

**Upgrade Call**

Yatra Online

Ongoing tensions in the Middle East (including limited use of air space by gulf countries and safety concerns) have led to travel disruption in the region. 

For YATRA, impact has been witnessing on its MICE segment. 

The 4Q is seasonally strong quarter for the MICE and gulf countries particularly UAE remain key destinations for corporate events.

MICE accounts for ~45% of the H&P bookings for YATRA and is a high margin business. 

Additionally, 4Q is seasonally a strong quarter for MICE and Middle East region accounts for 25-30% of total business for YATRA

Mankind Pharma Plans Early Semaglutide Launch in India

Mankind Pharma aims to be among the first companies to launch semaglutide in India after the drug’s patent expires on March 20.

India will be the primary launch market, while international markets will be explored later.

The company plans to launch the drug independently, with no co-marketing or partnership arrangements.

Semaglutide is widely used for diabetes and weight management, and demand is rising amid growing obesity and metabolic health concerns.

Mankind plans to price the drug carefully to balance affordability and long-term sustainability, continuing its strategy of introducing affordable medicines.

The company expects strong market expansion, as the patent expiry could allow around 50 generic brands to enter the Indian market.

Beyond diabetes, semaglutide has potential applications across cardiology, orthopaedics, and gynaecology, expanding its therapeutic scope.

**Marico Ltd — Sector Update | YES Securities | Mar-26**

**Analyst Call**
- Rating: **ADD** | Target Price: **₹850** (from ₹820)
- CMP: **₹752** | Upside: **~13%**

**Business Transformation**
- Shifting from legacy FMCG → **digital-first consumer powerhouse**
- New businesses to form **~33% of India revenues** by FY30
- Digital brands topline target: **₹40 Bn** by FY30
- Key digital brands: **Beardo (5x)**, **Plix (6x)**, **4700BC**, **Cosmix**

**Raw Material Tailwind**
- Copra down **14.8% QoQ** & **41% below recent peak**
- Easing copra + stable edible oil + packaging costs = **benign input environment**
- Management guiding **calibrated price corrections** to support volume recovery
- Margin expansion guided: **150–200 bps** in near-to-medium term

**Financial Projections (FY25–28E)**
- Revenue CAGR: **13.4%** | EBITDA CAGR: **13.6%** | PAT CAGR: **~15%**
- EPS: **₹12.5 (FY25) → ₹18.2 (FY28)**
- EBITDA margin: **17.2% (FY26E) → 19.8% (FY28E)**
- PAT margin: **12.7% (FY26E) → 14.8% (FY28E)**

**Return Ratios — Best in Class**
- ROE: **42.8% (FY26E) → 50.0% (FY28E)**
- RoIC: **75.6% → 89.8%**
- D/E: **~0.1x** — clean balance sheet

**Valuation**
- Trading at **56x/47x/41x** FY26E/FY27E/FY28E EPS
- EV/EBITDA: **41.2x/34.9x/30.4x**
- Premium to **Dabur & HUL** — justified by superior growth engines
- TP based on **~50x Sept'27E EPS** vs 5-year avg of **48.3x**

**Other Key Data**
- 1-year return: **+24.1%** | 52-week range: **₹593–₹814**
- Dividend yield: **1.4%**
- Promoter holding: **58.9%** | FII+DII: **36.4%**

**Key Takeaway**
- RM tailwind + digital growth = **double-digit earnings visibility**
- Margin recovery path is **clear & near-term**
- Digital portfolio scaling fast — **structural re-rating candidate**
- Clean balance sheet with **best-in-class return ratios**

@LongTermAlpha

**Home Decor Sector — Raw Material Shock | Nuvama Sector Update Mar-26**

**The Big Picture**
- **US–Iran conflict** disrupting Middle East energy & chemicals
- Near-term **margin pressure** across pipes, tiles & wood panels
- Broad-based **price hikes now inevitable**

**PVC & Plastic Pipes**
- PVC resin up **47% since Jan-26** → **₹101/kg**
- Crude up **43%** to **$86.7/bbl**
- Some manufacturers **stopped taking orders** due to price volatility
- **Q4FY26** expected to be strongest quarter on volumes & EBITDA/tonne
- Inventory losses of **9MFY26 reversing into gains**

**Jal Jeevan Mission — Residual Demand**
- JJM outlay: **₹8.7 Tn** | Rural tap coverage: **17% → 82%**
- High pending states: **Rajasthan (41%)**, **WB (43%)**, **Jharkhand (45%)**, **Kerala (45%)**, **MP (26%)**
- Residual JJM work offers **further demand upside** for pipe players

**Ceramics — Severe Stress**
- Gujarat Gas cut Morbi supply to **50%** from March 6
- **~100 Morbi units** already shut
- Up to **70% factories** could shut if propane stops
- **~1,500 export containers** stuck due to Hormuz tensions
- War-risk surcharge: **$4,000–5,000/container** vs normal **$300–600**
- Profit/container **~$300** — margins completely **wiped out**

**Wood Panels — Cost Inflation**
- Methanol **+46%** | Formaldehyde **+35%** | Melamine **+25%**

**Why SIPs Thrive in Crashes: 2008 Proof**  

**Peak vs. Dip Reality**  
- **Jan 2008 Peak (NAV ₹16.84)**: ₹10K SIP → **79K** (XIRR 12.8%), **593 units**.  

**Power of Falling NAVs**  
- NAV crashes to **₹12, 9, 7, 6, 5** → SIP buys **~1,000 units** (more shares at bargain prices).  

**Dip Installments Win Big**  
- **2009 Bottom SIP**: ₹10K → **₹2.8L** (₹2.7L gain vs. ₹69K at peak).  
- **Similar holding periods**, but **exponential unit accumulation** drives superior returns.  

**Strategy Edge**  
- **Continue SIPs**: Rupee cost averaging maximizes long-term compounding.  
- **Legends top up**; **ultra-legends lump sum** on deep dips.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Falling markets = **SIP superpower**—buy more units cheap, ride the rebound for outsized gains like 2008’s 28x bottom returns. Stay invested.

- **Kotak Bank, CSB Bank** have high capitalization but have kept payouts low.

**Key Takeaway:**  
**Prioritize dividend upside in HDFC/PSUs over buybacks — ROE strength favors payout hikes in a capped repurchase environment.**

CLSA On Smartphones

BROKERAGE RADAR

Indian smartphone volumes remain under pressure, down 25% YoY/QoQ in January

Believe could be attributed to memory price and availability concerns, driving 8% ASP increase since September

Motorola, Transsion, Oppo and Realme, key customers of Dixon, have seen 20%-68% year-on-year volume decline

This could raise concerns on meeting FY26 guidance and FY27 growth estimates

Pricing pressure remains accentuated for entry-level phones on memory price rises

Getting enough memory allocation remains a priority for cloud service providers versus smartphones and PCs

Easing of Press Note 3 approvals and HKC JV are positive, but are already in consensus estimates

Easing of memory prices and consummation of Vivo JV are the key catalysts to watch

New growth avenues would be crucial for Dixon in the medium term

Jefferies On Banks

BROKERAGE RADAR

Evaluating Buyback Optionality Among Indian Banks

In 2025, buybacks by global banks doubled to >$260 billion aided by higher ROE, capital and slow growth

Indian banks' ROE has risen & growth has slowed, but is still above peers

So we see limited scope for buybacks, but with hike in cap on dividend, some can raise payout

HDFC, Union Bank, and Bank of India have higher capital and payout

Kotak Bank and CSB have higher capitalisation, but have kept payouts low
Iran 🇮🇷 has ~10× the population and far larger geography, yet Israel’s 🇮🇱 economy is bigger and its income per person is ~10× higher.

**India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap**

• **NBFCs share:** ~21% of total credit  
• **Banks share:** Majority of remaining credit

• **Core issue:** Not a capital shortage, but a **credit translation problem**

• **Credit gap:** MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face **₹25–30 trillion deficit**

• **Economic role:** These segments contribute **~30–40% of India’s GDP**

• **Employment:** Employ **majority of India’s non-farm workforce**
**Strategic mapping of top transformer players in India**

**SAMHI Hotels – PL Capital Event Update Highlights**

**Rating, Target Price & Upside**
• **Rating:** BUY maintained
• **CMP:** ₹147
• **Target Price:** ₹264 (earlier ₹290)
• Valuation based on **10.5x FY28E EBITDA** (earlier 12x)
• **Implied upside:** ~79%

**RARE India Acquisition**
• SAMHI to acquire **70% stake in RARE India** for **~₹474 Mn**
• Structure: **₹234 Mn primary + ₹240 Mn secondary investment**
• Post-deal ownership: **SAMHI 70% | Founders 30%**

• RARE portfolio: **67 hotels, ~990 rooms**
• Presence across **15+ Indian states plus Nepal & Bhutan**
• Properties include **forts, palaces, hill retreats, wildlife lodges & boutique hotels**

• Business model: **Asset-light experiential leisure platform**
• Typical **ARR ~₹25,000 per night**

**Marriott Tie-up & B2C Expansion**
• RARE to become **Marriott’s exclusive platform for “Outdoor Collection by Marriott Bonvoy”**
• Coverage across **India, Nepal, Sri Lanka & Bhutan**

• Current model: **B2B subscription**
• Fees: **₹0.2–0.4 Mn per hotel annually**
• Plus **18–20% booking commission**

• B2C stack largely **untapped so far**

• Current portfolio enterprise revenue: **~₹2,500 Mn**
• Based on **~35% occupancy & ~₹20,000 ARR**

• Expected improvements post-Marriott integration:
• **Occupancy:** ~35–45% → ~50–55%
• **ARR uplift:** ~15% over time

• Expansion pipeline:
• Currently **67 hotels**
• Active talks with **25–30 properties**
• Near-term target **~90–100 hotels**
• Medium-term target **~120–150 hotels**
• Room inventory target **~1,800–2,200 rooms**

• B2C revenue forecast:
• **FY27E:** ₹425 Mn
• **FY28E:** ₹556 Mn
• **FY29E:** ₹709 Mn

**Financial Impact**
• **EBITDA estimates raised ~4%** for FY27E & FY28E
• Driven by **new B2C commission revenue**

• Expected growth (FY26E–FY28E):
• **Revenue CAGR:** ~15%
• **EBITDA CAGR:** ~23%

**Key Financial Projections (SAMHI)**

• **Revenue**
• FY25: ₹11,205 Mn
• FY26E: ₹12,974 Mn
• FY27E: ₹14,868 Mn
• FY28E: ₹17,279 Mn

• **EBITDA**
• FY25: ₹4,071 Mn
• FY26E: ₹4,708 Mn
• FY27E: ₹5,796 Mn
• FY28E: ₹7,126 Mn

• **EBITDA Margin**
• ~36.3% (FY25–26)
• ~39.0% FY27E
• ~41.2% FY28E

• **Adjusted PAT**
• FY25: ₹872 Mn
• FY26E: ₹1,361 Mn
• FY27E: ₹2,656 Mn
• FY28E: ₹3,242 Mn

• **EPS**
• ₹3.9 → ₹6.2 → ₹12.0 → ₹14.7
• EPS growth **95.1% in FY27E**
• EPS growth **22.1% in FY28E**

**Balance Sheet Improvements**
• Total debt: **₹21,285 Mn (FY25) → ₹12,785 Mn (FY28E)**
• **Net debt/equity:** **1.8x → 0.3x**

• **RoE:** 8.0% → 12.9%
• **RoCE:** 9.1% → 14.3%

**Valuation Snapshot**
• **EV/EBITDA:** 7.2x FY27E | 5.8x FY28E
• **EV/Sales:** 2.8x FY27E | 2.4x FY28E
• **P/E:** 12.2x FY27E | 10.0x FY28E
• **P/B:** 1.4x FY27E | 1.2x FY28E

**RARE Platform Economics**
• Expected **RoCE:** ~60–70%
• Payback period: **~18–24 months**

• As network scales to **~120–125 hotels**
• Portfolio enterprise revenue potential: **~₹8,000 Mn**

• EBITDA potential:
• Current B2B model: **~₹150 Mn**
• Post B2C transition: **~₹315–400 Mn**
• Operating margin potential: **35–40%**

**Key Positives**
• Entry into **high-ARR experiential luxury segment**
• **Asset-light expansion model**
• **Marriott partnership** boosts occupancy & pricing
• Improving **balance sheet & return ratios**

**Key Watchpoints**
• Execution of **B2C platform rollout**
• **Integration with Marriott ecosystem**
• Ability to scale to **120–150 hotels**
• Demand cyclicality in **premium leisure travel**
